results.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Latin American markets flooded by ...
results.snippet: More than 80% of the over 61,000 EVs sold in 2024 in Brazil were Chinese brands, predominantly BYD and GWM. In Mexico, sales of Chinese-made ...
results.summary:
HONG KONG (AP) — Affordable Chinese electric vehicles and inexpensive e-commerce products are increasingly making their mark in Latin American nations such as Brazil and Mexico, prompting concern among local governments and industries.
Latin America holds a strategically vital position for China as Beijing strengthens its connections with rapidly developing markets like Brazil and Chile.
Facing sluggish domestic demand due to an economic slowdown, Chinese automakers and manufacturers are now setting their sights on international consumers.
Countries like Mexico, Brazil, and Chile have begun implementing measures to restrict certain inexpensive Chinese imports in an effort to safeguard their local industries.
...
With their prices undercutting competitors due to substantial government subsidies and low production costs, Chinese automotive brands are rapidly penetrating the Latin American market.
In Brazil, over 80% of the more than 61,000 electric vehicles sold in 2024 were from Chinese manufacturers, primarilyD andWM.
Mexico, of vehicles produced in China represented around15% the domestic last year as reported by a Mexican automotive industry association, contrasting sharply with the U.S. approach, which has effectively barred Chinese vehicles through significant tariffs.
...
China's industries are keen on Latin America's abundant natural resources, ranging lithium in to copper Chile andmeal in Peru.
However, the region is experiencing expanding trade deficits with China, which saw its global surplus soar to a historic $1.2 trillion last year.
...
Research from ING Bank indicates that China's exports to Mexico surged by about 150% from 2017 to 2024, with shipments of vehicles and auto parts more than tripling.
To defend local industries, Mexico has introduced tariffs as high as 50% on imports from China, covering automobiles, appliances, and clothing.
Brazil isasing outde minim' import exemptions for overseas packages valued under $50, targeting inexpensive imports from China, and is also raising tariffs on electric vehicle imports.
Chile has increased tariffs and implemented 19 value-added on low parcels as of October.

results.title: Brazil B2C Ecommerce Market Report 2025-2029: Mercado Livre ...
results.snippet: Marketplace penetration remains high, with Mercado Livre, Amazon, Magalu, Americanas, Shopee, and Via dominating traffic and fulfillment.
results.summary:
-
...
The ecommerce market in Brazil is expected to grow by 9.9% annually, reaching US$64.09 billion by 2025.
The ecommerce market in the country has experienced robust growth during 2020-2024, achieving a CAGR of 17.8%.
This upward trajectory is expected to continue, with the market forecast to grow at a CAGR of 8.6% from 2025 to 2029.
By the end of 2029, the ecommerce market is projected to expand from its 2024 value of US$58.31 billion to approximately US$89.18 billion.
...
Brazil's e-commerce sector is shaped by a concentrated set of large multi-vertical platforms competing on logistics speed, payments integration, and seller acquisition.
Marketplace penetration remains high, with Mercado Livre, Amazon, Magalu, Americanas, Shopee, and Via dominating traffic and fulfillment.
Competition has intensified as cross-border platforms, particularly Shopee, Shein, and AliExpress, expand their presence.
Their focus on low-ticket categories and aggressive pricing continues to put pressure on domestic operators.
...
Physical retail networks are increasingly used as fulfillment hubs, supporting same-day or next-day delivery in major urban centers.
**Key Players and New Entrants**
Mercado Livre remains the largest marketplace, supported by Mercado Pago and Mercado Envios.
Amazon continues to expand Prime benefits and logistics assets, using faster delivery as its primary differentiator.
Shopee and Shein have maintained momentum in fashion and low-value general merchandise, supported by cross-border sourcing models.
...
Newer entrants include Temu, which began testing the Brazilian market in late 2024, and several domestic logistics-first players that provide last-mile services to marketplace sellers.
...
Mercado Livre has continued its logistics investments, expanding regional distribution centers and lowering free shipping thresholds.
...
- Mercado Livre has been cutting shipping costs and, in mid-2025, dropped the free-shipping threshold in Brazil to R$19, covering almost the entire catalog, explicitly to counter Shopee and Temu in low-ticket categories.
...
- Cross-border e-commerce, especially low-ticket imports from Chinese platforms like Shopee, Shein, AliExpress and Temu, has grown quickly in recent years.
Brazil is now moving from an incentive and regularization phase (Remessa Conforme) to more stringent enforcement and new tax rules on small parcels, directly affecting price-sensitive cross-border flows.
...
- On the omnichannel side, retailers like Magazine Luiza and Via Varejo use physical stores as mini-distribution centers, enabling same-day or next-day delivery and click-and-collect options, which a 2025 logistics analysis highlights as a key enabler of Brazil's omnichannel retail.

results.title: Brazil opens new shipping route to China via Amapá Port
results.snippet: Brazil opens new shipping route to China via Amapá Port ... logistics costs by more than 30%, positioning Amapá as a strategic trade corridor.
results.summary:
Brazil opens new shipping route to China via Amapá Port
Direct connection between Santana and Zhuhai cuts logistics costs by more than 30%, positioning Amapá as a strategic trade corridor
Published: Aug 31, 2025 07:43 PM
...
By Brasil 247 - Brazil formally joined a new international maritime trade route on Saturday (Aug 30) with the inauguration of a direct link between the Port of Santana, in the northern state of Amapá, and the Port of Gaolan in Zhuhai, China, Agência Gov reported.
**Strategic boost for Brazil-China trade**
According to Integration Minister Waldez Góes, the new connection offers decisive logistical advantages that will improve Brazil's competitiveness in global markets.
"This inauguration is the result of the Brazilian government's ongoing cooperation agenda with China.
The route brings mutual benefits—facilitating Chinese imports that strengthen local industry while opening new channels for Brazilian agricultural and bioeconomy exports," Góes said.
He emphasized that the new route could cut transportation times by up to 30 days and reduce logistics costs by more than 30%.
**Expanding Brazil-China cooperation**
Fábio Hu, president of the Brazil-China International Development Chamber of Commerce, highlighted the business opportunities the route opens.
...
The new shipping line represents a strategic step in deepening Brazil's economic ties with China, its largest trading partner.
For Amapá, it raises the state's profile in Amazon and Midwest logistics networks, positioning it as a key link between global trade flows and the region's bioeconomy.

results.title: Takeaways from report on Chinese goods flooding Latin American ...
results.snippet: More than 80% of the over 61,000 EVs sold in 2024 in Brazil were Chinese brands, predominantly BYD and GWM. In Mexico, sales of Chinese-made ...
results.summary:
HONG KONG (AP) — Affordable Chinese electric vehicles and inexpensive e-commerce products are increasingly making their way into Latin American nations such as Brazil and Mexico, raising concerns among local governments and industries.
Latin America holds significant strategic importance for China as Beijing seeks to strengthen connections with rapidly expanding markets like Brazil and Chile.
In response to sluggish domestic demand, Chinese automakers and manufacturers are turning their attention to international markets.
Countries like Mexico, Brazil, and Chile have initiated actions to limit certain inexpensive Chinese imports, aiming to safeguard their own industries.
...
Chinese automotive brands are swiftly entering the Latin American market, benefiting from substantial government subsidies and reduced production costs that allow them to offer lower prices than competitors.
In Brazil, over 80% of the more than 61,000 electric vehicles sold in 2024 were from Chinese manufacturers, primarilyD andWM.
Mexico, of vehicles produced in China made up approximately 15% of the local market last year, highlighting a stark contrast to the U.S., which has implemented significant tariffs to block Chinese vehicles from its market.
…
...
China relies on Latin America's abundant natural resources, including lithium from Brazil, copper from Chile, and fishmeal from Peru, to feed its manufacturing needs.
However, trade deficits with China have been escalating throughout the region, with its global surplus reaching an unprecedented $1.2 trillion last year.
Between January and October 2025, Mexico's trade deficit with China hit $101 billion, while Argentina's deficit approached $8.2 billion last year.
Research from ING Bank indicates that Chinese exports to Mexico surged by approximately 150% from 2017 to 2024, with shipments of cars and auto parts more than tripling.
To shield their local industries, Mexico has introduced tariffs as high as 50% on imports from China, covering automobiles, appliances, and clothing.
Brazil is in the process of eliminating or gradually phasing out 'de minimis' tax exemptions on overseas packages valued under $50, partly targeting inexpensive imports from China.
Additionally, Brazil is raising tariffs on electric vehicle imports.
Starting in October, Chile has increased tariffs and implemented 19 value-added on low packages.

results.title: Chinese e-commerce platforms in the ranking of the top ten in Brazil
results.snippet: Shopee, Temu, Shein and AliExpress, along with South Korea's Samsung, together recorded 639 million accesses in January 2025, according to ...
results.summary:
Five of the ten largest e-commerce platforms in Brazil are Asian, and four of them are Chinese.
Shopee, Temu, Shein and AliExpress, along with South Korea's Samsung, together recorded 639 million accesses in January 2025, according to Conversion's “E-commerce Sectors in Brazil” report.

results.title: New direct maritime route between Brazil and China to boost ...
results.snippet: The new direct service could cut the journey between countries by 30 days and reduce logistics costs by more than 30%.
results.summary:
This week marked the official inauguration of the direct maritime route between the Port of Gaolan (Zhuhai), in southern China, and the Brazilian ports of Santana (AP) and Salvador (BA).
The new direct service could cut the journey between countries by 30 days and reduce logistics costs by more than 30%.
China’s ambassador to Brazil, Zhu Qingqiao, said both countries will continue to deepen cooperation and that the new route is part of the agreements signed by the presidents of Brazil and China.
...
“This route is exactly what grape and melon producers needed to start exporting to China,” Coelho said.
“We are very excited about this new phase that will transform the presence of Brazilian fruit production in the Asian market.”
The association stated that the new route reflects the strengthening relationship between Brazil and China—Brazil’s main trading partner—which has intensified following the official visit of Chinese President Xi Jinping to Brazil at the end of 2024.
Last year, Brazil’s Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock (MAPA) and China’s General Administration of Customs (GACC) signed protocols establishing phytosanitary requirements for the export of fresh table grapes, sesame, sorghum, and fish derivatives from Brazil to China—the official opening of the Chinese market to Brazilian table grapes.
Most of Brazil’s grape exports will come from the states of Pernambuco and Bahia.

results.title: Brazil-China trade soars 50-fold from 2001 to 2024 - LinkedIn
results.snippet: In a dramatic shift, given their status as leading trading partners, US imports from China fell by more than 35% between January and July 2025 ...
results.summary:
China-Brazil bilateral trade soared 50-fold from 2001 to 2024.
China is hungry for the output of the South American giant's soybean farms, mines, ranches and forests; meanwhile, Brazil is buying ever more Chinese value-added goods – with a recent boom in electric-vehicle imports.
We have charted key inflection points.

results.title: Up 30% YoY: Shopee's Breakout Year in Brazil Is Just Beginning
results.snippet: The report projects Shopee's GMV in Brazil to reach BRL 70 billion in 2025, up from roughly BRL 50 billion in 2024 — representing close to 30% ...
results.summary:
According to the latest report released by BTG Pactual, Brazil’s e-commerce gross merchandise value (GMV) reached approximately **BRL 330 billion in 2024** and is expected to grow by **15% in 2025**, pushing the market close to **BRL 380 billion**.
…
...
In BTG Pactual’s 2025 outlook, **Shopee stands out as the most important variable** in Brazil’s e-commerce landscape.
The report projects Shopee’s GMV in Brazil to reach **BRL 70 billion in 2025**, up from roughly **BRL 50 billion in 2024** — representing close to **30% year-on-year growth**.
To put this into perspective, Brazil’s overall e-commerce market is growing at around **15% annually**.
...
Today, **over 90% of Shopee Brasil’s GMV comes from local sellers**.
...
## Logistics as the Core Competitive Moat
Shopee’s confidence in targeting **BRL 70 billion GMV** in 2025 is rooted in what it has quietly built beneath the surface: a highly localized logistics network.
Shopee is currently the **second-largest warehouse tenant in Brazil**, operating **more than 13 major distribution centers** alongside hundreds of sorting hubs nationwide.
Through **Shopee Express**, its proprietary logistics arm, the company has established dense “capillary-level” delivery coverage across the country.
…
## Amazon’s Capital Offensive and Temu’s Price Shock
...
**Amazon** has shown unprecedented ambition from late 2024 into 2025.
To secure high-quality supply, Amazon introduced **zero-commission onboarding policies** in Brazil and waived large portions of FBA storage fees.
BTG analysts view this as a capital-driven growth strategy aimed at squeezing both local incumbents and fast-growing platforms like Shopee.
…
## Industry Outlook: Localization Determines the Winners
...
Through **3 million local sellers**, automated domestic fulfillment centers powered by **Shopee Express**, and deep cultural integration, **Shopee Brasil** has evolved from a foreign entrant into a genuinely local e-commerce platform.

results.title: China to Vitória, Brazil: A Comprehensive Shipping Guide
results.snippet: This guide outlines sea and air freight options, logistics services, and key considerations for efficient transportation to this South American destination.
results.summary:
### China to Vitória, Brazil: A Comprehensive Shipping Guide
Planning to ship goods from China to Vitória, Brazil—a vibrant port city and industrial hub in the state of Espírito Santo?
This guide outlines sea and air freight options, logistics services, and key considerations for efficient transportation to this South American destination, supporting trade across manufacturing, agriculture, and energy sectors.
**Sea Freight Services**
Vitória’s
**Port of Vitória**is a major gateway for cargo to southeastern Brazil, handling both direct and transshipped goods.
Below are key routes from Chinese ports, including direct services and transshipment via major hubs like Santos (Brazil) or Miami (USA):
|Port of Origin|Carriers & Transit Time|Transshipment Hubs|Inland Transit to Vitória|
|--|--|--|--|
|Shenzhen|CMA CGM (38–45 days)|Santos, Miami|~2 hours by truck from port|
|Qingdao|ONE (42–48 days)|Hamburg, Santos|Reliable coastal road network|
|Ningbo|MSC (40–46 days)|Rotterdam, Santos|Coordinated with local agents|
|Xiamen|ZIM (36–42 days)|Singapore, Santos|Expert management of Amazonia routes|
|Shanghai|Yang Ming (35–40 days, fastest)|Los Angeles, Santos|Direct trucking from port to city|
**Service Highlights**:
**FCL/LCL Options**: Full-container loads (FCL) for bulk shipments; less-than-container (LCL) with warehouse consolidation in Chinese ports.
**Direct Routes**: Shanghai and Shenzhen offer the fastest direct sea freight (35–45 days) to Santos, with onward transport to Vitória.

results.title: Trade value between China and Brazil 2024 - Statista
results.snippet: In 2024, Brazil's exports to China exceeded its imports by roughly 25.2 billion U.S. dollars. In that year, the value of products exported from ...
results.summary:
In 2023, Brazil's exports to China exceeded its imports by roughly 48.3 billion U.S. dollars.
In that year, the value of products exported from Brazil to the Asian country reached nearly 105 billion U.S. dollars, an increase of about 14.6 billion U.S. dollars in comparison to the previous year.
Meanwhile, imports totaled approximately 56 billion U.S. dollars.
## Imports and exports of Brazil with China from 2015 to 2023 (in billion U.S. dollars)
